utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all 5 corresponding sections. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: the format requires each coverage type to include costs. It does not require provider details beyond that. Health: has plan name and cost -> satisfies 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'; it explicitly states Physical & Mental in name. Property: has plan name, details, and cost -> satisfies 'home/contents insurance with costs'. Travel: has plan name, details, and cost -> satisfies 'travel insurance with costs'. Additional services: includes service, details, and cost (including discount) -> satisfies 'any enhancement services with costs'. Total budget: includes monthly and annual cost breakdown -> satisfies 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'. Therefore, all items are present and fully specified per the requested format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists four items: LifeFirst Standard Plan (Physical & Mental Health) under health_coverage, EliteHome Premium Coverage under property_coverage, PrimeTravel Premium Plan with Adventure Travel Coverage under travel_coverage, and Premium Insurance Membership under additional_services. From the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan appears in both primary health physical (rating 9) and mental health (rating 4). Since the plan states Physical & Mental Health together, we must map both items and their respective ratings. EliteHome Premium Coverage is found under property_protection.home_content with rating 2. PrimeTravel Premium Plan is found under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance with rating 3; Adventure Travel Coverage is a separate travel_enhancement_service with rating 10, so both should be mapped. Premium Insurance Membership is in general_services with rating 6.
